content:
Learning by Building Real Projects

2 min read
Just now

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

image owned by authorFrom writing my first lines of HTML to building full-stack projects with Next.js and APIs, here’s how hands-on learning shaped my journey into web development.
Hi, I’m Aditya Sharma (Aadi), a BCA student and aspiring full-stack developer from India.
I’m currently learning web development, JavaScript, React, APIs, UI design, SEO, and data structures & algorithms.
My journey started with curiosity. At first, coding felt confusing. HTML, CSS, JavaScript, GitHub, and deployment were all new concepts. But over time, I realized one simple truth:
You learn coding by building, not just by watching tutorials.
That realization changed the way I approached learning.
Instead of only consuming tutorials, I started creating real projects.
I built applications like GitAura, a GitHub profile analyzer, and a Weather App powered by live APIs. I’m also developing my personal portfolio website to showcase my projects, skills, and learning journey.
These projects taught me lessons that tutorials couldn’t — debugging real issues, improving user experience, working with APIs, and writing cleaner code.
My goal is to become a skilled software engineer who builds products that are fast, useful, and thoughtfully designed.
I’m still learning, and I know there’s a long road ahead. Every project, bug, and challenge helps me improve.
This is just the beginning of my journey as a student developer — building one project at a time.
